projects.title: MedCare
projects.description: AI-powered multilingual health chatbot on WhatsApp.
projects.longDescription: A robust public healthcare chatbot built to bridge translation barriers. Connected to the WhatsApp Business API using FastAPI, powered by the Google Gemini model to deliver medical guidance, dynamic triage diagnostics, and emergency contact mappings in multiple local languages.

story.problem: Public healthcare centers in rural regions face severe language translation barriers, preventing patients from communicating their symptoms accurately or receiving prompt triage guidance. Standard web applications require high-speed internet and digital literacy, which are often absent.
story.solution: Developed a zero-setup WhatsApp chatbot using FastAPI that communicates in local dialects. By integrating Google's Gemini LLM with custom prompt constraints, the system handles multilingual queries, translates symptoms on the fly, maps them to basic triage tiers, and offers localized health education.
story.architecture: The WhatsApp Business API sends user messages via webhooks to a FastAPI microservice running inside a Docker container on AWS. FastAPI routes the incoming payload, validates state caches in Redis, queries the Gemini API with structured system prompts, and responds back through WhatsApp.
story.challenges: Faced high latency in LLM completions which timed out the WhatsApp webhook receiver (requires response under 5s). Solved this by decoupling the message receiver from the LLM execution thread using Python's asyncio tasks, immediately acknowledging the WhatsApp webhook, and sending the LLM reply asynchronously.
story.impact: Simulated deployments demonstrated rapid symptom-to-triage sorting in under 10 seconds. Supports over 5 local languages without requiring complex apps.
story.learning: Gained deep experience in asynchronous Python code, managing webhook response window limitations, and designing fail-safe prompt templates for safety-critical medical contexts.

projects.title: EcoFi
projects.longDescription: An environmental tech platform utilizing computer vision models to identify and categorize solid waste. Leveraged deep learning APIs to suggest optimized recycling workflows, reward user recycling streaks, and simulate regional carbon-footprint reduction statistics.

story.problem: Solid waste segregation at source is highly inefficient due to public confusion over recycling rules, leading to clean recyclables ending up in landfills and causing severe environmental issues.
story.solution: EcoFi provides a simple mobile-friendly web interface where users take a photo of an item, and an integrated TensorFlow computer vision model instantly labels it (e.g., PET Plastic, Cardboard, Glass) and details the proper local recycling workflow.
story.architecture: A React frontend queries a FastAPI backend. The backend hosts a lightweight TensorFlow Lite model for waste classification. Classification results are saved to MongoDB, which calculates points and user streaks to encourage recycling habits.
story.challenges: Model inference on resource-constrained host servers was slow. We optimized the system by converting our custom TensorFlow model to TensorFlow Lite and enabling client-side image compression in React before uploading, reducing payload size by 80%.
story.impact: Helped users catalog over 1,200 recycling items with a 92% accurate image identification rate, rewarding consistent streak holders with local sponsor vouchers.
story.learning: Mastered model optimization techniques like quantization, client-side canvas processing, and structure designs for document databases.

projects.title: URL Shortener SaaS
projects.description: Enterprise URL management tool with custom analytics.
projects.longDescription: A fully functional URL redirection and analytics software. Provides real-time click-tracking, geographic referrals, and browser-agent distribution analytics through high-performance caching layers and responsive analytics charts.

story.problem: Enterprise marketing campaigns require dynamic, short URLs with instant geo-tracking analytics, but third-party services are expensive, have high redirection latencies, and compromise user data privacy.
story.solution: Created a lightweight, self-hosted URL Shortener SaaS. It uses next-generation caching strategies to deliver redirections in under 15ms while tracking referral headers, user-agents, and location metadata in the background.
story.architecture: Next.js App Router serves as the frontend and API gateway. Short links are stored in PostgreSQL but cached inside Redis with an eviction policy. When a user requests a short URL, Next.js checks Redis first (O(1) lookup), redirects the user instantly, and triggers a background database job to record analytics.
story.challenges: Writing real-time analytical click records directly to PostgreSQL during high traffic spikes caused database thread pool exhaustion. We resolved this by queueing click logs in a Redis List stream and running a cron worker to batch-insert them into PostgreSQL every 30 seconds.
story.impact: Achieved redirections under 15ms. Managed up to 500 requests per second with minimal PostgreSQL resource utilization.
story.learning: Developed strong proficiency in Redis cache-invalidation strategies, PostgreSQL indexing for time-series logs, and database write batching.

projects.title: Secure DB Connection Framework
projects.description: Microservice library for authenticated SQL pooling.
projects.longDescription: A custom Python framework engineered to secure microservice data connections. Implements automated credential rotation, connection pooling, and SSL-handshake validation layers to mitigate SQL-injection vulnerabilities.

story.problem: Connecting dozens of microservices to a centralized SQL cluster safely is challenging. Hardcoded credentials, plain-text connection strings, and lack of SSL enforcement frequently expose databases to eavesdropping or breach.
story.solution: Built a Python-based database connection client library that enforces encrypted connection handshakes, dynamically retrieves database passwords from secure secret managers with automated rotation, and handles thread-safe pooling.
story.architecture: A packaged library importable in Python microservices. It intercepts initialization parameters, connects to AWS Secrets Manager using IAM authentication to pull connection strings, sets up thread-safe SQLAlchemy connection pools, and enforces TLS 1.3 certificates.
story.challenges: Query latency increased during secrets rotation checks. We implemented a local token-bucket cache that stores credentials in memory for 10 minutes with strict cryptographic salting, eliminating frequent network roundtrips to the Secrets Manager.
story.impact: Successfully integrated in 4 internal microservices, ensuring zero hardcoded database secrets and eliminating plain-text transmission risks.
story.learning: Gained a deep understanding of cryptography algorithms (AES-256), SSL/TLS handshakes, connection pool lifecycle hooks, and safe database access controls.
